text.title: Database Selection Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply the database-selection methodology end-to-end.

step.name: profile-workload
step.action: Synthesize a workload profile: read/write ratio, query shape, consistency tier, scale envelope.

step.name: shortlist-candidates
step.action: Build a candidate set of 3-5 DBs covering each plausible engine family.
step.decision-gate: If only 1 candidate fits, expand the candidate set.

step.name: score
step.action: Score each candidate against 4 criteria (access fit, consistency, scale, ops fit) on 1-5; sum totals.

step.name: spike
step.action: Run a 1-week benchmark with realistic data + the 3 hottest queries; measure p95 latency + throughput.
step.decision-gate: If neither spike meets SLO, return to step 2.

step.name: draft-adr
step.action: Author selection ADR with ≥2 rejected alternatives + rollback path.

step.name: tag-reversibility
step.action: Apply adr-reversibility-tagging; DB selection is almost never two_way_door.
step.decision-gate: If reversibility comes out as one_way_door_irrevocable, escalate to founder sign-off + 48h cool-off.
